title: Executive Order 12832—Amendments Relating to the National Research Council

flags.justification: The National Research Council's functions are advisory and research-coordinating in nature ('stimulate,' 'survey,' 'promote cooperation,' 'gather and collate'), not concentrated regulatory power.
Sec. 3 explicitly subjects the Academy to OMB Circular A-122 cost principles and any further requirements the OMB Director specifies.
Multiple related functions coherently serve one purpose: the Council's research-coordination charter.
An advisory and research-coordination charter, not a compliance regime against third parties.
No compliance regime is created by this order.
A standing charter for a permanent institution established in 1916 and still operating today.
This adds explicit financial-accountability requirements (OMB Circular A-122 compliance) rather than removing any existing oversight.

Sec. 4's requirement that non-competitive acquisition be justified by the Academy's unique, otherwise-unavailable qualifications is a real, bounding criterion, though not itself a third-party compliance regime.

Updates the charter of a historically significant, still-operating institution: the principal operating arm of the National Academy of Sciences, National Academy of Engineering, and Institute of Medicine.
No second agency's competing mandate is implicated.

The non-competitive-acquisition provision is bounded by a specific, narrow criterion (the Academy must be the only source offering unique, necessary qualifications), not a broad blanket exemption from competitive bidding.

notes: Validation batch (negative). Structural weight: 1/12 = 8.3%. President George H.W. Bush's final executive order, issued on his last full day in office — updating the charter of a permanent scientific institution dating to 1918, a fittingly low-key close to his term. Notably, this is the same calendar date, January 19, as EO 12279, President Carter's final full-day order twelve years earlier, though the two could hardly differ more in gravity.
